description: The spec asked to run analyse() inside a Web Worker via DOMParser, but DOMParser is [Exposed=Window] and does not exist in Worker scope.
resolution: Run analyse() on the main thread (native DOMParser, namespace-aware, zero parser deps). Yield once via setTimeout(0) before parsing so a loading state paints. For very large files, the upgrade path is a fast-xml-parser worker; pretty-print/DOM work must stay on main thread.

description: prettyXml() called XMLSerializer.serializeToString(el) on every element just to read its opening tag, but that re-serializes the whole subtree. On a 25MB file the line diff hung for minutes.
resolution: Build the opening tag directly from el.tagName + attributes (O(1) per element => O(n) total). Also render the line diff non-blocking (setTimeout) and collapse long unchanged runs to a fold row, capped at 20k rows, or the DOM alone freezes the browser.

description: Tried to surface undefined namespace prefixes as a soft warning (undefinedNamespaces). But per XML namespaces spec an undeclared prefix is a well-formedness error, so DOMParser rejects the whole document - it can never be a warning on an otherwise-valid file.
resolution: Detect the parsererror and throw XmlParseError with line/column + the offending prefix. Do not expect undefinedNamespaces to populate on a parseable doc.

content: Spec lists 1/0 under boolean, but integer is checked first so bare '1'/'0' resolve to integer (a 0/1/2 column reads integer, not boolean). Only word forms true/false/yes/no are boolean.
